reasoning: Based on Wikipedia's current FIDE ranking page as of our cutoff (June 22, 2025), Aleksandra Goryachkina is officially ranked exactly #8 among women with a rating of 2533. For her ranking to remain "as high or higher" (Rank 1 through 8), she needs to either keep her rank or move up. For June 29, the Wikipedia rankings will still reflect the June 1 FIDE official list, guaranteeing she remains Rank 8 (estimated at 0.98). For July 22, this depends on the July 1 list. Since there appear to have been no significant events for top female players in June, her rank should remain 8th (0.94). For September 20 and December 19, the FIDE Women's World Cup taking place in July (and other Fall tournaments) will influence rankings. Goryachkina benefits from an asymmetrical rating distribution: she is only 2 points behind #7, 10 points behind #6, and 13 points behind #5, but she has a massive 18-point cushion over #9 (Kateryna Lagno at 2515). This "rank cushion" means that average rating fluctuations are significantly more likely to push her into the top 7 or keep her at #8, than to drop her down to #9 or lower. Furthermore, as a historical 2600+ player, she has positive structural upside from a suppressed 2533 rating. Thus, she is very likely to remain in the top 8 through the end of the year, with uncertainty increasing moderately over time.
